texto: The philosophers make still another objection: "What you gain in rigour," they say, "you lose in objectivity. You can rise toward your logical ideal only by cutting the bonds which attach you to reality. Your science is infallible, but it can only remain so by imprisoning itself in an ivory tower and renouncing all relation with the external world. From this seclusion it must go out when it would attempt the slightest application.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Henri Poincaré sobre temas ligados a autoestima. From this seclusion it must go out when it would attempt the slightest application.

texto: Consider now the Milky Way. Here also we see an innumerable dust, only the grains of this dust are no longer atoms but stars; these grains also move with great velocities, they act at a distance one upon another, but this action is so slight at great distances that their trajectories are rectilineal; nevertheless, from time to time, two of them may come near enough together to be deviated from their course, like a comet that passed too close to Jupiter. In a word, in the eyes of a giant, to whom our Suns were what our atoms are to us, the Milky Way would only look like a bubble of gas.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Henri Poincaré: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Consider now the Milky Way. Here also we see an innumerable dust, only the grains of this dust ar…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, astronomy, atoms pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e atoms.

texto: Pure analysis puts at our disposal a multitude of procedures whose infallibility it guarantees; it opens to us a thousand different ways on which we can embark in all confidence; we are assured of meeting there no obstacles; but of all these ways, which will lead us most promptly to our goal? Who shall tell us which to choose? We need a faculty which makes us see the end from afar, and intuition is this faculty. It is necessary to the explorer for choosing his route; it is not less so to the one following his trail who wants to know why he chose it.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Henri Poincaré que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados.
